results.identified_issue: Steelman had mic recording for optional audio files but no speech-to-text, so spoken answers never became text for analysis/Discover TTS. ATG uses the open-source speech_to_text Flutter package, which on iOS is Apple SFSpeechRecognizer (with an onDevice flag). Matched that approach natively: OnDeviceSpeechTranscriber wraps SFSpeechRecognizer + AVAudioEngine live buffers, sets requiresOnDeviceRecognition when supportsOnDeviceRecognition is true, taskHint dictation, partial results into the answer TextField. Did not pull WhisperKit or a third-party model—user pointed at ATG and said open-source on-device STT for now, with upload/cloud STT later. Removed the old audio-only AVAudioRecorder path from SubmitAnswerView for this pass so the primary loop is dictate→text→analyze; Answer.audioFileName / Discover file playback remain for future upload work. Unverified on device: first-run speech permission sheet, on-device model download availability on a specific iPhone, and long dictation stability.
results.summary: Added on-device speech-to-text dictation on the Answer tab
results.detailed_description:
Looked at ATG: it uses the speech_to_text package (Apple Speech under the hood on iOS). Steelman now does the same thing natively.

What changed:
• New OnDeviceSpeechTranscriber — live dictation via SFSpeechRecognizer + AVAudioEngine. When the device supports it, recognition is forced on-device so audio is not uploaded. Partial results stream into the Answer text field; “Dictate more” appends to existing text.
• Answer tab — replaced pure “Record audio” with Dictate (on-device STT). You still type if you want; submit needs text (typed or dictated). Discover continues to play text answers with TTS.
• Info.plist / project.yml — added NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription for the system permission prompt.

Not in this pass: cloud upload STT or keeping a separate voice-file attachment path. That can land when you want audio upload. Please try Dictate on a device (needs Speech + Mic permission) and send feedback if on-device fails to start or accuracy is off.

description: First of all, we need a way better speech to text model. Look at our ATG repo and you can see we've used a speech to text model there. There's one in open source on device speech to text model. So let's go with that for now. And then eventually we'll probably need to do uploading audio and all that. But for now, a speech to text is good.
